candidates.foreshadow_summary: Montresor produces a trowel from under his cloak as a sign of being a mason, which Fortunato dismisses as a jest.
candidates.payoff_summary: Montresor uses the same trowel to wall up the entrance of the niche, entombing Fortunato alive.

proposed_trigger.description: Montresor starts using the trowel for masonry
observable_conditions: P0074: 'With these materials and with the aid of my trowel, I began vigorously to wall up the entrance of the niche.'

candidates.relation_description: The trowel, initially presented as a joke, becomes the instrument of murder.
candidates.stage1_rationale: The trowel appears in a seemingly playful context and is later repurposed with deadly intent, spanning a significant narrative gap.

candidates.foreshadow_summary: The narrator declares that revenge must be accomplished without risk to himself and the victim must know who is avenging him.
candidates.payoff_summary: Fortunato, realizing his fate, cries out 'For the love of God, Montresor!' acknowledging Montresor as his murderer, while Montresor goes unpunished.

observable_conditions: P0082: 'For the love of God, Montresor!'

candidates.relation_description: The specific rules of revenge set at the very beginning are fulfilled when the victim identifies his killer at the climax.
candidates.stage1_rationale: The opening philosophical statement directly maps to the climactic recognition, demonstrating delayed fulfillment across the entire narrative.

candidates.foreshadow_summary: Montresor lures Fortunato with the promise of a pipe of Amontillado, exploiting his wine expertise.
candidates.payoff_summary: After chaining Fortunato, Montresor ironically echoes 'The Amontillado' and begins walling him in, revealing the lie; there is no wine.

proposed_trigger.description: Montresor stops pretending about the Amontillado and starts masonry
observable_conditions: P0074: 'As I said these words I busied myself among the pile of bones... I soon uncovered a quantity of building stone and mortar.'

candidates.relation_description: The false promise of wine leads to a fatal trap, and the payoff is the unmasking of the lure.
candidates.stage1_rationale: The central deception is set up early and paid off when the truth is revealed through action rather than explanation, creating dramatic irony.
